vulnerability.title: Disable Core Dumps for All Users
vulnerability.description: To disable core dumps for all users, add the following line to /etc/security/limits.conf, or to a file within the /etc/security/limits.d/ directory: * hard core 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q pam; }; then SECURITY_LIMITS_FILE="/etc/security/limits.conf" DROPIN_DIR="/etc/security/limits.d" DROPIN_FILE="$DROPIN_DIR/10-ssg-hardening.conf" REGEX_CORRECT_VALUE="^\s*\*\s+hard\s+core\s+0\s*$" # Remove bad configuration in drop-ins if [ -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ]; then for override in "$DROPIN_DIR"/*.conf; do if [ -f "$override" ] && ! grep -qE "$REGEX_CORRECT_VALUE" "$override"; then sed -ir -E '/^[[:space:]]*\*[[:space:]]+hard[[:space:]]+core[[:space:]]+/ s/^/#/' "$override" fi done fi if [ -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ] && grep -qEr "$REGEX_CORRECT_VALUE" "$DROPIN_DIR"; then exit 0 elif [ ! -d "$DROPIN_DIR" ] && grep -qE "$REGEX_CORRECT_VALUE" "$SECURITY_LIMITS_FILE"; then exit 0 else mkdir -p "$DROP

triage_decision.reason: Disabling core dumps is a standard hardening practice that prevents sensitive information from being written to disk during a process crash. The remediation involves adding a configuration line to /etc/security/limits.conf or a drop-in file, which is a low-risk configuration change that does not impact system availability or connectivity.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: Loss of debugging information for application crashes
